description: Enhance classify_unpinned() to differentiate risk: actions/* and github/* (first-party, lower risk if unpinned) vs third-party (higher risk). Still flag all unpinned, but severity differs. First-party tags are less likely to be hijacked but still vulnerable (tj-actions proved tags are mutable). OWASP CICD-SEC-3.
title: Differentiate first-party vs third-party unpinned actions

close_reason: Replaced all 3 python3 JSON parsing calls with gh api --jq. Removed python3 as runtime dependency. Updated docs, tests, and preflight checks.
description: Lines 435-440: Three python3 -c invocations for JSON field extraction. gh api already supports --jq which is used elsewhere in the script. Refactoring to use --jq eliminates the python3 runtime dependency entirely. Related: python3 preflight check becomes unnecessary.
title: Replace python3 JSON parsing with gh api --jq

description: Since HDF v2 is now the native internal format, this card just adds the --hdf FILE CLI flag to write the assembled HDF v2 JSON to disk. Minimal work — the data is already in HDF v2 structure, just serialize and write.
title: Add --hdf output flag to write HDF v2 file

close_reason: Use wf_uncommented for pull_request_target and issue_comment trigger detection in analyze_repo(). Added fixtures prt-in-comment.yml and ic-in-comment.yml with 4 new tests.
description: Lines 254, 274, 331: Bare substring grep matches pull_request_target, issue_comment, and permissions: in YAML comments, run: block strings, and name: fields. Mitigation: strip lines starting with # before pattern matching (grep -v '^\s*#'). Full fix requires YAML parsing but comment stripping catches the most common false positives.
title: Reduce comment/string false positives for trigger detection

close_reason: Closed
description: Line 286-287: Guard regex only matches single-quoted actor checks (e.g., == 'dependabot). GitHub Actions expressions commonly use double quotes too. Also, github.actor == 'dependabot[bot]' is not detected in PRT context (only user.login is checked). Line 336 checks actor but only for issue_comment. Fix: accept both quote styles, add actor check to PRT classifier.
title: Fix author guard detection — support double quotes and github.actor

close_reason: Fixed: repo list error handling exits with crit, Phase 4 single API call, analyze_repo writes directly to files.
description: Lines 501-505: analyze_repo outputs two lines (md + csv) split by head -1 / tail -1. This breaks if any cell contains a newline. Options: (1) write directly to temp files from within analyze_repo (pass file paths as args), (2) use NUL delimiter, (3) validate line count before splitting. Option 1 is cleanest -- eliminates the fragile two-line protocol entirely.
title: Refactor analyze_repo output to eliminate fragile head/tail split

description: Grep for 'secrets: inherit' in workflows using workflow_call. This passes ALL repository secrets to called workflows, violating least privilege. If the reusable workflow is compromised, every secret is exposed. Trivial single grep. OWASP CICD-SEC-6.
title: Detect secrets: inherit in reusable workflow calls

close_reason: Renamed TABLE_ROWS→TABLE_ROWS_FILE, TABLE_ROWS_CSV→TABLE_ROWS_CSV_FILE. Initialized detail/detail_csv before if/elif chains.
description: Rename TABLE_ROWS to TABLE_ROWS_FILE (it's a path, not an array). Clarify prt/ic abbreviations (prt_workflows_md, ic_workflows_md or similar). Standardize SCREAMING_CASE for config/paths, lowercase for runtime data. Initialize detail and detail_csv before if/elif chains.
title: Improve variable naming consistency

description: After writing the report file, print a brief summary to stdout: repos scanned, critical findings count, high findings count, report file path. Currently the user gets [INFO] messages but no findings summary on the terminal. Should show something like: 'Scanned 42 repos: 3 critical, 7 high, 12 medium findings. Report: ./my-org-actions-audit.md'
title: Print terminal summary after run

description: New classifier for workflows writing to GITHUB_ENV, GITHUB_PATH, or GITHUB_OUTPUT files. Any value written becomes an env var or PATH entry for ALL subsequent steps. If untrusted data is written, attacker achieves RCE. Grep for >> $GITHUB_ENV, >> $GITHUB_PATH, >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT in run: blocks. Higher severity when combined with github.event.* expressions. Real-world: Google Firebase, Apache exploits. OWASP CICD-SEC-4.
title: Detect GITHUB_ENV/GITHUB_PATH/GITHUB_OUTPUT injection

description: Ship as a gh CLI extension so users can install with 'gh extension install rapidfort/gh-actions-audit' and run 'gh actions-audit my-org'. Requirements: (1) Repo named gh-actions-audit (or alias/fork). (2) Root executable named gh-actions-audit (no .sh). (3) Shell-based extension — no manifest needed, gh auto-detects. (4) Users get: one-line install, runs from anywhere, 'gh extension upgrade' for updates. (5) Zero new dependencies — we already require gh. (6) Keep tests/CI working with renamed script. (7) Consider publishing to gh extension registry for discoverability. Research saved in .beads/ from agent. Pairs well with x6s (gh CLI conventions).
notes: Research saved: .beads/research-gh-extension-conventions-2026-03-04.md. Key takeaways: (1) Repo must be named gh-<name>, executable at root named gh-<name> (no .sh). (2) No manifest needed for bash extensions. (3) Add gh-extension topic to repo for discoverability. (4) gh extension install . for local dev (symlink, instant edits). (5) Popular examples: meiji163/gh-notify (324+ stars) — just a single bash file at root.
title: Package as gh CLI extension

close_reason: grep -F → awk for secret lookup, extracted find_workflow_files(), raised limit to 99999, added --cleanup+--local warning.
description: The same find expression appears 3 times (lines 267, 415, 543): find DIR -type f \( -name '*.yml' -o -name '*.yaml' \). Extract to a helper function for DRY and easier future changes (e.g., supporting .yml.j2).
title: Extract find_workflow_files() helper

close_reason: Closed
description: A workflow with 'echo This uses pull_request_target' in a run: block is flagged as having a pull_request_target trigger. The current comment stripping (grep -v '^\s*#') only handles full-line comments, not string values in run: blocks. Create fixture prt-in-string-value.yml and ic-in-string-value.yml. Consider checking if the keyword appears in the on: section specifically rather than anywhere in the file.
notes: [2026-03-04 03:30] RED phase complete. Test confirms false positive exists: trigger keywords in run: string values match as triggers. Fixture: trigger-keyword-in-run.yml. Fix: extract on: section with awk, check triggers only there. Files: gh-actions-audit.sh, test/test_workflow_analysis.bats, test/fixtures/workflows/trigger-keyword-in-run.yml
title: Fix false positive: trigger keywords in run: string values

description: When --exit-code is passed, exit with non-zero status if critical or high findings are detected. Useful for CI pipelines that want to fail a build if the org has security issues. exit 0 = clean, exit 1 = findings detected. Default behavior (no flag) is always exit 0.
title: Add --exit-code flag for CI integration

close_reason: Removed sleep 0.1 — gh api handles its own rate limiting and retry. The sleep just slowed scans unnecessarily.
description: Line 223: sleep 0.1 per repo adds ~100ms * repo_count of dead time (20 seconds for 200 repos). gh CLI handles rate limiting via retry headers. Either remove the sleep or replace with rate-limit header inspection. Add a comment if intentionally kept.
title: Remove or justify sleep 0.1 in download loop

description: Add --repo REPO flag to audit a single repo instead of the whole org. Useful for quick checks or PR gates. Downloads only that repo's workflows. Still requires ORG positional arg for API paths.
title: Add --repo flag for single-repo audit

description: The /orgs/{org}/actions/permissions endpoint has two useful fields we don't report: sha_pinning_required (new Aug 2025, boolean — org enforces SHA pinning) and enabled_repositories (all/none/selected — which repos can use Actions). Both are free to add since we already call the endpoint. Add to Phase 4 org settings section.
title: Report sha_pinning_required and enabled_repositories org settings

description: Replace pipe-delimited temp file output with HDF v2 JSON generation. analyze_repo() produces per-requirement results. Phase 5 assembles wrapper with all repo objects + org-level object. MD/CSV renderers read from HDF structure instead of pipe-delimited strings. Add json_escape() helper. Keep bash 3.2 compat — use printf templates.
notes: [2026-03-04 05:30] Started: json_escape() and emit_hdf_requirement() implemented with tests. Next: wire emit_hdf_requirement into analyze_repo(), build wrapper structure, update MD/CSV renderers. Files: gh-actions-audit.sh, test/test_hdf_output.bats
title: Refactor output to HDF v2 native format

close_reason: All 6 children closed: fork ref detection, author guard fixes, secret matching, comment stripping, checkout false positives.
description: Sub-epic: Fix false positives/negatives in YAML workflow analysis patterns. Includes fork ref detection, author guard matching, secret name matching, and comment stripping. Depends on function extraction (DRY epic) so changes are in isolated, testable functions.
title: Grep heuristic accuracy improvements

description: The create/delete events fire when branches/tags are created. github.event.ref (branch/tag name) is fully attacker-controlled. Add to classify_expr_injection() dangerous_pattern. NOTE: may be covered by auu (expand expression injection patterns) — merge if so. OWASP CICD-SEC-4.
notes: May be covered by auu (expand expression injection patterns). Check if auu covers this before starting.
title: Detect missing dangerous expression: github.event.ref

close_reason: Closed
description: The mock gh script hashes all args to find canned responses. But Phase 4 calls include --jq flags that are not included in mock_gh_response registrations. The hash mismatch means mock falls through to default exit 0 with empty output, and the || { warn; var=unknown } fallback catches it. Phase 4 report values are effectively untested. Fix: register mock responses with full arg list including --jq. Add assertions for org settings values in report.
title: Fix mock gh CLI hash mismatch for Phase 4 tests

close_reason: Replaced 10 echo-pipe patterns with here-strings (<<<) in analyze_repo and download loop. Eliminates unnecessary subshell forks.
description: Lines 194, 364, 436-440: echo $var | cmd forks unnecessary subshells. Use here-strings: cmd <<< $var. Lines 185, 275: basename in loops — use ${var##*/} parameter expansion. Lines 319-324: $(printf ...) subshells for string concat — use direct assignment. Lines 244-246: while-read-append — use mapfile.
title: Replace echo-pipe with here-strings and eliminate unnecessary forks

close_reason: Closed
description: Line 285: Pattern only matches pull_request.head.(sha|ref). Misses github.head_ref (equivalent in PRT context) and pull_request.head.repo.full_name (used with actions/checkout repository: param). These are common dangerous checkout patterns classified as low-risk. Add to the regex.
title: Fix fork ref detection — add github.head_ref and head.repo.full_name

close_reason: Single-pass file-based secret mapping replaces O(N×M) grep loop. Uses temp file instead of associative array for bash 3.2 compatibility. Also lowered bash requirement from 4.0 to 3.2 (stock macOS).
description: Line 415: grep -rl runs across all workflow files for EACH org secret. For 20 secrets and 500 files = 10,000 file reads. Instead: read all files once, extract secrets.XXX references, build declare -A associative array mapping secret names to repos. Reduces to O(files) from O(secrets*files).
title: Single-pass secret-to-repo mapping with associative array

close_reason: Added classify functions, fixtures, and tests for all three detection types. Integrated into analyze_repo with new report columns.
description: Detect runs-on: self-hosted or custom labels (not ubuntu-*/macos-*/windows-*). Self-hosted runners are persistent machines -- compromised runners give attackers access to host network and cached credentials. Critical when combined with pull_request_target, issue_comment, or workflow_run triggers. Flag in report with severity note.
title: Detect self-hosted runner usage

description: When no args given, show full usage (all options, not just the abbreviated line). --help already works via awk extraction of the comment block. Fix the no-args case to match.
title: Improve --help and no-args usage output

close_reason: Replaced 4 sort-pipe-while loops with process substitution (< <(sort FILE)) to avoid subshell variable scoping.
description: Lines 514, 546, 623, 636: sort FILE | while read runs the while body in a subshell. Not a bug today (loops only append to files) but fragile. Use: while read ... done < <(sort FILE) for consistency with the rest of the script and to avoid future variable-scoping surprises.
title: Use process substitution for sort-pipe-while loops

close_reason: Extended expression injection to detect dispatch inputs, fixed progress line clearing.
description: Detect workflow_dispatch inputs and repository_dispatch client_payload used in run: blocks via ${{ inputs.* }} or ${{ github.event.client_payload.* }}. These are injection vectors when repos have broad contributor access. Lower risk than expression injection (requires repo write access) but still a privilege escalation path.
title: Detect workflow_dispatch and repository_dispatch input injection

description: classify_prt and classify_ic both produce detail|detail_csv where values differ only in markdown bold (**...**). Several branches have identical values. Return a single canonical value and use a strip_md() helper (sed 's/\*\*//g') to produce the CSV form. Reduces maintenance burden when adding new classifications.
title: Return single value from classify functions, strip markdown at call site

close_reason: All 8 children closed: extracted require_arg, join_array_cells, classify_prt, classify_ic, analyze_repo, handle_cache_cleanup. Eliminated code duplication, improved naming, used process substitution. 77 tests passing.
description: Sub-epic: Extract functions (analyze_repo, classify_prt, classify_ic, json_get, join_cells), eliminate code duplication, improve variable naming. Depends on security fixes being done first since they change the same code.
title: DRY refactoring and function extraction

close_reason: Use wf_uncommented for all classify_prt checks (checkout, fork_ref, guards). Also fixed classify_ic actor check. Added fixture + 3 tests.
description: Line 284: grep -q 'actions/checkout' matches inside comments like '# - uses: actions/checkout@v4 # disabled'. The workflow gets classified as having a checkout when it doesn't. Same mitigation as comment stripping for trigger detection.
title: Detect commented-out actions/checkout as false positive

close_reason: Added classify functions, fixtures, and tests for all three detection types. Integrated into analyze_repo with new report columns.
description: Add detection for workflow_run trigger, which bypasses fork PR restrictions. Sub-classify: workflow_run + download-artifact = HIGH risk (artifact poisoning, used in Ultralytics YOLO attack), workflow_run + checkout = MEDIUM risk, workflow_run alone = LOW risk. Add classify_wfr() function following classify_prt pattern. Add new column or merge into trigger analysis.
title: Detect workflow_run trigger and artifact poisoning

close_reason: grep -F → awk for secret lookup, extracted find_workflow_files(), raised limit to 99999, added --cleanup+--local warning.
description: Three minor fixes: (1) Raise gh repo list --limit from 1000 to 99999 for large orgs (google, microsoft, aws have 1000+ repos). (2) Warn when --cleanup is combined with --local (cleanup is silently ignored). (3) Remove dead code: 'True' (capital T) check in can_approve_prs comparison -- jq always returns lowercase 'true'.
title: Raise repo list limit and add --cleanup + --local warning

close_reason: Remediation cmd built once in Phase 3 (5th field in ORG_SECRETS_FILE). Reuse cmd built once before interactive/non-interactive branches.
description: Lines 548-553 vs 637-643: Remediation command built twice (md + csv). Lines 686-691 vs 697-702: Reuse command echoed in both interactive/non-interactive branches. Build once, format for output context.
title: Deduplicate remediation command and reuse-command output

close_reason: Closed
description: Zero tests for CSV output generation. The csv_field() function (lines 772-781) handles quoting for commas, double quotes, and newlines -- none tested. Also csv_field is defined inside an if block so cannot be extracted via _script_preamble. Tests needed: (1) --csv flag produces valid CSV, (2) CSV has header row, (3) csv_field quotes commas, (4) csv_field escapes double quotes, (5) CSV has both per-repo and org-secrets sections separated by blank row.
title: Add CSV output tests and csv_field() unit tests

description: Associate each detection check (prt, ic, unpinned, expr injection, workflow_run, self-hosted, dangerous perms, org secrets) with the relevant CCI (Control Correlation Identifier) and NIST 800-53 controls. Add a mapping table to README or TESTING.md showing: detection → CCI → NIST control (e.g., unpinned actions → CCI-003161 → CM-7, expression injection → CCI-001310 → SI-10). Tag bats tests with the CCI reference in comments. This enables compliance reporting and connects findings to auditable control requirements.
notes: [2026-03-04 05:30] CCI mappings complete in hdf-profile/requirements.yaml. 10 CCIs mapped across 28 requirements. Verified against https://cyber.trackr.live/api/cci. Still blocked by 55h for integration into output.
title: Map tests and detections to CCI and NIST 800-53 controls

description: Add missing dangerous expression contexts to classify_expr_injection(): github.event.ref (create/delete triggers), github.event.head_commit.message, github.event.head_commit.author.name, github.event.head_commit.author.email, github.event.discussion.title, github.event.discussion.body, github.event.pages[].page_name. Easy: extend existing dangerous_pattern regex. OWASP CICD-SEC-4.
title: Expand expression injection patterns

close_reason: Closed
description: Create the HDF v2 baseline profile for gh-actions-audit. Each of our 28 detection checks becomes a requirement with: stable ID (GHA-001–028), STIG-style title/check/fix text, impact score, CCI tags, NIST 800-53 mappings, OWASP CI/CD category. Output: hdf-profile/requirements.yaml consumed by the renderer. Replaces the old 'define JSONL schema' plan — HDF v2 IS the schema.
notes: [2026-03-04] Created hdf-profile/requirements.yaml with 28 requirements (GHA-001–028), CCI mappings verified against API, STIG-style title/check/fix text, impact scores, OWASP CI/CD categories. Profile definition complete.
title: Define HDF v2 profile with requirement catalog

close_reason: All 4 sub-epics closed: ki2 (security), 8lf (DRY), 5ad (heuristics), cot (performance). 80 tests, all passing.
description: Epic: Comprehensive refactoring of gh-actions-audit.sh based on 4-agent code review covering DRY, security/robustness, performance/idioms, and grep heuristic accuracy. Must be completed before test suite implementation.
title: Script refactoring and hardening

close_reason: Extracted join_array_cells() function. Replaces ~20 lines of duplicate array-to-cell joining in analyze_repo(). 4 unit tests added.
description:
Lines 315-325 and 346-356: Identical pattern for joining arrays into markdown (
separated) and CSV (; separated) cells. Extract join_cells() function taking array, md separator, csv separator. Eliminates ~20 duplicated lines.
title: Extract array-to-cell join helper function

close_reason: Closed
description: Add detection for expression injection, unpinned actions, workflow_run, self-hosted runners, dangerous permissions values, and other missing patterns identified in the 4-agent review.
title: [EPIC] Detection coverage expansion

close_reason: Extended expression injection to detect dispatch inputs, fixed progress line clearing.
description: Three issues: (1) printf '%-80s' ' ' for clearing progress is a magic number that doesn't work on wider terminals -- use printf '\r\033[K' or tput. (2) Reuse command in handle_cache_cleanup doesn't quote paths with spaces. (3) paste delimiter inconsistency: line 485 uses ', ' (comma-space) while lines 561/571 use ',' (comma only). Standardize.
title: Fix progress line clearing and reuse command quoting

close_reason: Extracted require_arg() function. Replaces 3 identical 5-line validation blocks with 1-line calls. 3 unit tests added.
description: Lines 74-94: Three identical 6-line blocks validating --local, --out, --csv arguments. Extract require_arg() function that validates next arg exists and does not start with -. Reduces to three 1-line calls.
title: Extract argument validation helper function

description: Enhance classify_prt() to specifically flag ref: parameters that reference github.event.pull_request.head.sha, head.ref, or PR number in checkout steps. This is the core pwn request pattern — checking out attacker-controlled code into a privileged context. Currently sub-classified but ref: param not specifically called out. OWASP CICD-SEC-4.
title: Detect untrusted ref checkout in PRT contexts

close_reason: All 5 children closed: sleep removal, here-strings, single-pass secrets, process substitution, single-file-read.
description: Sub-epic: Replace repeated greps with single file read, single-pass secret mapping, modern bash idioms (here-strings, mapfile, parameter expansion). Can be done in parallel with heuristic fixes after DRY refactoring.
title: Performance and bash idiom improvements

close_reason: Fixed: repo list error handling exits with crit, Phase 4 single API call, analyze_repo writes directly to files.
description: Line 282: gh repo list failure is silently swallowed by 2>/dev/null. If the API fails (network drop, auth expiry, rate limit), the script proceeds with 0 repos and produces a silently empty report. This is a security tool -- missing data is worse than a crash. Fix: exit with crit() if gh repo list returns non-zero. Also track download failures in Phase 1 loop and warn if repos failed to download.
title: Add explicit error handling for gh repo list API failure

description: Heuristic: grep for common deployment patterns (docker push, aws, gcloud, kubectl, terraform apply) and check for absence of environment: key. Without environment protection rules, any automated event can deploy to production. High false-positive risk — make informational. OWASP CICD-SEC-1.
title: Detect missing environment: protection for deployments

close_reason: Added warn() calls to all gh api sites: user identity, repo secrets, org secrets, org permissions (2 calls). Silent fallbacks replaced with explicit warnings.
description: Lines 193, 204, 215, 360, 387, 435, 439: All gh api calls use 2>/dev/null || true, making HTTP 403, 404, network errors, and rate limiting indistinguishable from 'no data'. At minimum, check exit codes and emit warn() messages so users know data is missing from the report.
title: Warn on API failures instead of silently swallowing

close_reason: Added python3 preflight check with command -v, test passes
description: Lines 160-168: Preflight checks verify gh CLI but not python3, which is a listed dependency. If python3 is missing, Phase 4 silently produces 'unknown' for all org settings. Add command -v python3 check alongside the gh check. (Or better: eliminate python3 dependency by using gh api --jq — see related DRY issue.)
title: Add python3 preflight check

close_reason: Closed
description: Lines 237, 385, 369: Script creates temp files via mktemp but has no trap handler. If killed (Ctrl-C, SIGTERM, set -e failure), files are orphaned in /tmp. Add: trap cleanup EXIT with a function that removes TABLE_ROWS, TABLE_ROWS.csv, ORG_SECRETS_FILE, and optionally AUDIT_DIR.
title: Add trap EXIT for temp file cleanup

close_reason: Added regex validation for ORG name [a-zA-Z0-9_-], prevents path traversal and injection
description: Line 104-111: ORG is used unsanitized in file paths, API URLs, sed patterns, and markdown output. GitHub org names are restricted to [a-zA-Z0-9_-]. Validate with: [[ $ORG =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ ]] || exit 1. Prevents path traversal, sed delimiter injection, and malformed API calls.
title: Add ORG name validation

close_reason: Refactored Phase 2 to single-pass per-file: read wf_content once, strip comments to wf_uncommented once, then use bash matching and grep on variable. Reduced from ~10 grep-on-file calls to 1 cat + grep pipeline per file.
description: Lines 254, 274, 284-288, 331-336: Each workflow file is grepped up to 10 times (permissions, PRT, checkout, fork ref, dependabot, author guard x2, issue_comment, author_association, actor). Read file content once into a variable, then use [[ $content == *pattern* ]] bash matching. Eliminates ~10 forks per file.
title: Read workflow files once into variable instead of 10 separate greps

description: Grep for if:.*always() and continue-on-error: true combined with secrets.* access in the same job/step. These patterns ensure steps run even after failure, which can be exploited to exfiltrate secrets after a security gate fails. OWASP CICD-SEC-4.
title: Detect if: always() and continue-on-error with secret access

description: Extend download-artifact detection beyond workflow_run contexts. Flag uses:.*download-artifact across ALL workflows, not just workflow_run. Artifacts are an untrusted boundary — downloading and executing artifact contents is equivalent to arbitrary code execution. Real-world: Rust artifact poisoning (Legit Security), ArtiPACKED (Unit 42). OWASP CICD-SEC-4.
title: Detect artifact trust boundary violations

close_reason: Added classify_unpinned() function, 4 fixtures, 14 tests (grep, unit, integration). New Unpinned Actions column in md+csv reports.
description: Detect actions referenced by mutable tag (uses: foo/bar@v4) vs immutable SHA (uses: foo/bar@692973e3d...). This was the exact vector in the tj-actions/changed-files compromise (23K+ repos). Implementation: grep for uses: lines without @[0-9a-f]{40}. Report as ratio per repo (e.g., '2/15 SHA-pinned'). Flag third-party unpinned as higher risk than actions/* unpinned. GitHub now supports SHA pinning enforcement via org policy (Aug 2025).
title: Detect unpinned action references (tag vs SHA)

close_reason: Added warning when gh repo list returns 1000 results (limit hit)
description: Line 193: gh repo list --limit 1000 silently truncates. If exactly 1000 results are returned, warn the user that repos may be missing. Consider using --paginate or multiple calls for large orgs.
title: Warn when gh repo list hits 1000 limit

close_reason: Added classify_expr_injection() with awk-based run: block extraction, 7 fixtures, 15 tests (grep, unit, integration). New Expression Injection column in md+csv reports. Detects dangerous github.event.* and head_ref in run: blocks, ignores env: block usage and comments.
description:
DESCRIPTION
Scan for ${{ github.event.* }} and ${{ github.head_ref }} interpolated directly into run: blocks. This is the #1 exploited GHA vulnerability class (Ultralytics, Nx, hackerbot-claw). README lists it as attack vector #3 but the tool doesn't detect it. Dangerous contexts: issue.title, issue.body, pull_request.title, pull_request.body, pull_request.head.ref, comment.body, review.body, commits[*].message, head_ref. Add classify_expr_injection() function following classify_prt/classify_ic pattern. Add new column to per-repo report table.

Parent epic: bb8 (Detection coverage expansion)
title: Detect expression injection in run: blocks

close_reason: Fixed author_association and permissions: grep to strip comment lines before matching. Also fixed permissions-in-comment false positive (same class of bug).
description: Line 288: grep -q 'author_association' matches the string anywhere in the file — comments, echo statements, logging, non-conditional references. A workflow that logs author_association but doesn't gate on it gets classified as 'has guard'. Mitigation: strip comment lines before matching, or require 'if:' on the same/preceding line.
title: Fix author_association false positives — require if: context

close_reason: Closed
description: Decision needed: should our JSONL intermediate format BE HDF v2, or should HDF v2 be one of many output renderers? Arguments for native HDF v2: single format, direct Heimdall import, compliance-ready. Arguments against: HDF v2 has a specific schema (profiles, controls, results) that may not map cleanly to our per-repo findings model — our data is org-level scanning, not per-system compliance. Research: read HDF v2 spec (saf.mitre.org), compare to our data shape, decide before implementing awb (JSONL schema). This decision blocks awb.
notes: [2026-03-04] Decision REVERSED: HDF v2 as native internal format (not just output). Our checks map 1:1 to HDF requirements. Wrapper for multi-repo/org scans. MD/CSV/XLSX are renderers from HDF, not separate paths.
title: Evaluate HDF v2 as native JSONL schema vs output format

close_reason: Extracted classify_prt() and classify_ic() with 6 unit tests. analyze_repo() now calls them, reducing its body further.
description: Lines 270-313 (PRT classifier) and 327-356 (IC classifier) are cohesive blocks that take a workflow file and return a classification string. Extract as separate functions for independent testability. Depends on analyze_repo() extraction.
title: Extract classify_prt() and classify_ic() functions

close_reason: Both children closed: zbm (workflow URLs) and lky (summary statistics).
description: Add summary statistics, workflow URLs, risk scoring, and improved report formatting.
title: [EPIC] Report quality and UX improvements

close_reason: grep -F → awk for secret lookup, extracted find_workflow_files(), raised limit to 99999, added --cleanup+--local warning.
description: Line 570: grep '^secret_name|' uses regex matching. GitHub secret names are [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]* so no metacharacters in practice, but defense in depth says use grep -F (fixed string) or awk with exact field match. Alternative: validate secret names from API response before use.
title: Use grep -F for secret name lookup in SECRET_MAP_FILE

close_reason: Fixed: repo list error handling exits with crit, Phase 4 single API call, analyze_repo writes directly to files.
description: Lines 597-604: The same endpoint orgs/ORG/actions/permissions/workflow is called twice with different --jq filters. Wastes an API call, doubles rate-limit cost, introduces inconsistency if one call fails. Fix: fetch once, extract both default_workflow_permissions and can_approve_pull_request_reviews from the same response. Also remove dead code: the 'True' (capital T) check on line 645 is unreachable since jq always returns lowercase 'true'.
title: Deduplicate Phase 4 API call (permissions/workflow endpoint)

description: Add --xlsx FILE flag to generate Excel spreadsheet output from HDF v2 data. Consider using python3 openpyxl (optional dependency) or CSV-to-XLSX conversion. Lower priority — CSV covers most spreadsheet use cases.
title: Add --xlsx output flag

close_reason: All 8 children closed: mktemp, trap EXIT, base64 portability, python3 preflight, ORG validation, API warnings, 1000 limit warning, while-read loops
description: Sub-epic: Fix temp file safety, add trap cleanup, input validation, error handling, and portability issues. These are foundational and must be done first as they change script structure.
title: Security and robustness fixes

close_reason: Fixed grep to use word boundary: secrets.$name([^a-zA-Z0-9_]|$) prevents FOO matching FOOBAR
description: Line 415: grep -rl 'secrets.$secret_name' matches secrets.FOO inside secrets.FOOBAR. This inflates the 'Referenced In Workflows' column and generates incorrect gh secret set --repos remediation commands. Fix: use word-boundary pattern like secrets\.$secret_name([^A-Za-z0-9_]|$) with grep -E.
title: Fix secret name partial matching — FOO matches FOOBAR

description: Grep for actions/cache or cache: in workflows triggered by pull_request from forks. Cache entries are shared across workflows/branches — a PR-triggered workflow can poison cache entries affecting main branch builds. Research: Adnan Khan Monsters in Your Build Cache. OWASP CICD-SEC-4.
title: Detect cache poisoning vectors

close_reason: Added clickable repo→workflows links, summary statistics table with per-check counts.
description: Add a summary at the top of the report with headline numbers: repos scanned, repos with no permissions blocks (count + %), repos with pull_request_target (by sub-classification), repos with issue_comment (no gate), org secrets with All visibility. Executives and security leads need the headline numbers without reading the full table.
title: Add summary statistics section to report

close_reason: Closed
description: Set up test infrastructure: bats-core with bats-assert/bats-support/bats-file as git submodules in test/test_helper/. Create test/test_helper/common.bash with shared setup (PATH stubs for gh, fixture paths). Create test/fixtures/workflows/ and test/fixtures/api_responses/ directories. Add .shellcheckrc. This must be done before any TDD work on the refactoring cards.
title: Set up bats-core test framework and CI pipeline

close_reason: Extracted handle_cache_cleanup() function. Reduces main script flow from ~45 lines to a single function call.
description: Lines 658-706: ~45 lines with multiple branches for cleanup, interactive prompt, and reuse command printing. Extract handle_cleanup() function to clarify main script flow.
title: Extract cleanup/reuse logic into function

close_reason: Closed
description: Fix silent API failures, fragile output protocols, untested code paths, and edge cases identified by QA review.
title: [EPIC] Robustness and error handling improvements

close_reason: Closed
description: Two low-effort checks: (1) Detect hardcoded tokens in workflow files (ghp_*, gho_*, AKIA* patterns) -- the SpotBugs attack started with a PAT in a workflow file. (2) Check whether repos use step-security/harden-runner -- it successfully detected the tj-actions compromise in real-time. Report as summary statistic: 'X/Y repos use harden-runner'.
notes: [2026-03-04 03:30] Not started yet. Need to add grep patterns for ghp_*, gho_*, AKIA* in workflow files + check for step-security/harden-runner usage.
title: Detect hardcoded secrets and harden-runner adoption

close_reason: Closed
description: Line 369: TABLE_ROWS.csv is created by string concatenation (appending .csv to mktemp output), not by mktemp itself. Predictable path enables symlink attacks on shared systems. Also line 122: AUDIT_DIR uses date-based naming, not mktemp -d. Create all temp files/dirs via mktemp.
title: Use mktemp for all temp files — fix predictable TABLE_ROWS.csv path

description: Detect usage of known-compromised actions: tj-actions/changed-files (CVE-2025-30066, 23K repos affected), reviewdog/action-setup (CVE-2025-30154, initial vector), dawidd6/action-download-artifact (<v6, fork artifact search). Maintain small blocklist, grep for uses:.*<action-name>, report version/SHA. Distinct from unpinned detection — flags specific bad actions regardless of pinning. OWASP CICD-SEC-3.
title: Add known-vulnerable action blocklist

description: Grep for ::set-output, ::save-state, ::set-env, ::add-path in run: blocks. These were deprecated because they are injection vectors — any action that prints to STDOUT can inject workflow commands. GitHub will disable these. Easy grep detection. OWASP CICD-SEC-4.
title: Detect deprecated workflow commands

close_reason: Closed
description: Line 217: base64 -d is GNU coreutils flag. Stock macOS uses -D. The 2>/dev/null || true masks the failure, so all workflow files end up empty on macOS. Detect the correct flag at script startup. This is the highest priority fix.
title: Fix base64 -d portability — fails silently on stock macOS

description: TDD: Create build_hdf_repo_target(repo, repo_dir) that runs all classifiers on fixture workflows and returns a JSON object with requirements[] array. Each classifier result maps to emit_hdf_requirement() using GHA-NNN IDs from requirements.yaml. Test against existing fixtures (permissions-none, prt-checkout-no-guard, etc). RED then GREEN.
title: Add build_hdf_repo_target() function with tests

description: Grep for common cloud credential secret names (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AZURE_CREDENTIALS, GCP_SA_KEY) WITHOUT id-token: write. Flag as 'Consider OIDC' recommendation. Also: reclassify id-token: write from dangerous to neutral/good (it enables OIDC federation, not a security risk). OWASP CICD-SEC-6.
title: Detect static cloud credentials instead of OIDC

close_reason: Extracted analyze_repo() function with all local variables. Loop body reduced from ~130 lines to 5. Function outputs pipe-delimited rows to stdout.
description: Lines 239-371: The for-repo loop body is ~130 lines with ~20 variables leaking into global scope. Extract into analyze_repo() to enable local scoping and testability. This is the foundational refactor — classify_prt and classify_ic extraction depend on this.
title: Extract analyze_repo() function from Phase 2 loop body

description: Since this is a GitHub Actions tool in the gh ecosystem, align with gh CLI conventions rather than generic GNU/POSIX. Reference: gh CLI source, gh extension docs, 12 Factor CLI. Key conventions: (1) --json flag with structured output (matches gh pr list --json). (2) --jq flag for filtering JSON output (matches gh api --jq). (3) --format for choosing output (table, json, csv). (4) Default to human-readable table output on TTY, JSON on pipe. (5) Respect GH_FORCE_TTY and NO_COLOR env vars. (6) Short flags for common options (-o/--out, -q/--quiet). (7) --version flag. (8) Exit codes: 0=success, 1=findings, 2=usage error. (9) Consider packaging as gh extension (gh extension install). (10) Support -- to end option processing. (11) 12-factor: prefer flags over args, help on no args.
title: Align CLI with gh CLI extension conventions

close_reason: Replaced for-in word splitting loops with while-read for repos and workflow files
description: Line 201: for repo in $all_repos — use while read loop. Line 214: for wf in $wf_list — same. Lines 149-153: Variables in printf format strings (SC2059). Line 672: rm -rf $AUDIT_DIR should use ${AUDIT_DIR:?}. These prevent breakage on repos/files with spaces or glob chars.
title: Fix unquoted word-splitting loops and printf format strings

close_reason: Added clickable repo→workflows links, summary statistics table with per-check counts.
description: Include clickable GitHub URLs to each flagged workflow file: https://github.com/ORG/REPO/blob/HEAD/.github/workflows/FILENAME. URL is deterministic from org/repo/filename. Saves reviewers significant time navigating to flagged files in large orgs.
title: Add workflow file URLs to report

close_reason: Added classify functions, fixtures, and tests for all three detection types. Integrated into analyze_repo with new report columns.
description: Currently the tool checks whether a permissions: block EXISTS but not what it GRANTS. A workflow with permissions: write-all is just as dangerous as no permissions block. Detect: write-all, contents:write, actions:write, packages:write, id-token:write. Change permissions cell from 'All (3/3)' to include dangerous grants like 'All (3/3) -- 1 write-all'. Also detect id-token:write combined with pull_request_target (OIDC token theft risk).
title: Detect dangerous permissions values (write-all, contents:write)

description: Add --json FILE flag to write HDF v2 JSON output. Replaces the old --jsonl plan since we're using HDF v2 as native format. Consider also supporting --json - for stdout piping. Aligns with gh CLI conventions (gh pr list --json).
title: Add --json output flag for HDF v2 JSON
